role: Senior MOIS officer; head of Iran's intelligence station in Albania (2014–2017), operating under diplomatic cover as first attaché
bio:
Fereydoun Zandi Aliabadi is a senior officer of Iran's [Ministry of Intelligence](/orgs/ministry-of-intelligence) (MOIS) who served as head of the intelligence station at the Iranian embassy in Tirana, Albania, from 2014 to 2017. Operating under the title of first attaché, Zandi managed a network of approximately 25 intelligence officers tasked with surveillance and operations against the [Mojahedin-e Khalq](/orgs/mojahedin-e-khalq) (MEK), whose members had relocated to Albania and were based at [Camp Ashraf-3](/orgs/camp-ashraf-3) near Manez.

Zandi coordinated closely with MOIS counterparts in Iraq to build agent networks targeting MEK members. He was replaced as station chief by [Mostafa Roudaki](/people/mostafa-roudaki) at the end of 2017. The subsequent expulsion of Iranian diplomats from Albania, beginning with Ambassador [Gholamhossein Mohammadnia](/people/gholamhossein-mohammadnia) in December 2018, was linked to the intelligence activities that Zandi and his successors had directed.

sources.title: Iran regime establishes anti-PMOI (MEK) 'intelligence station' in Albania
Unmasking Details on Demonization Campaign, Espionage, Terrorist Plots by the Iranian Regime's Ministry of Intelligence, Embassy in Albania

editorial_notes: MOIS station chief in Albania 2014–2017, replaced by Mostafa Roudaki end of 2017. Operated under first attaché cover.
